nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Loundres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Loundres was born in 1354, in Athelhampton, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Agnes Martin Loundres, of Ogmoor. He died in 1383, in Dorset, England, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Loundres est né en 1354, à Athelhampton, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Agnes Martin Loundres, of Ogmoor. Il est décédé en 1383, à Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 29 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Loundres wurde 1354 in Athelhampton, Dorset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Agnes Martin Loundres, of Ogmoor mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1383 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Dorset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Loundres è nato il nel 1354 nel Athelhampton, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Agnes Martin Loundres, of Ogmoor. Morì nel 1383, nel Dorset, England, all'età di 29 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Loundres родился в 1354 г. в местности ателхамптон, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Agnes Martin Loundres, of Ogmoor. Он умер в 1383 г. в местности дорсет, Англия в возрасте 29 лет.
